page: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 384 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with a dedicated allocation, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. This configuration provides high-density compute capabilities for workloads requiring direct hardware access and high-frequency execution.

The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the server includes 22,800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, offering high-speed, low-latency local data access directly bundled with the hardware. It does not include any graphics processing units (GPUs).

Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps, supporting high-throughput data transfers. Given the combination of high-frequency dedicated Intel CPUs, massive local NVMe SSD storage, and high-speed networking, this bare-metal instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-performance computing (HPC) applications that require direct physical hardware control without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance utilizing Intel x86_64 architecture. It delivers 384 dedicated vCPUs across 192 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio, prioritizing raw processing power. Storage is highly robust, featuring 22,800 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, while network performance is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth. With no integrated GPUs, this instance is tailored for non-accelerated, high-throughput workloads. The bare-metal design eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for large-scale databases, high-performance computing, and intensive data processing tasks that demand direct hardware access and massive local storage capacity.
